economic_notes: Lil'wat Nation has over 2,000 members in the Pemberton Valley, BC. Business enterprises include Lil'wat Holdings Limited Partnership (99.99% owned), Lil'wat Construction Enterprises LP, Lil'wat Forestry Ventures LP, Lil'wat Retail Operations LP, Lil'wat Management Services LP, and multiple numbered BC Ltd. companies (50% owned). Gas station construction project underway. BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing agreement. Whistler Blackcomb Master Development Agreement. BC Hydro settlement funds internally restricted. Baxter Creek Developments investment sold. COVID-19 noted as subsequent event.
notes: Financial statements are non-consolidated (unaudited supplementary schedules attached). A separate consolidated set of financial statements was also prepared. Business enterprises (Lil'wat Holdings LP and related entities) are accounted for on a modified equity basis — total investment $21.66M. Long-term debt includes both capital leases ($294,137) and long-term mortgage/loan debt ($9,276,403). Net financial assets of $19,850,457 per Statement of Change in Net Financial Assets. Total government transfers calculated as ISC + CMHC + DFO + FNESC + FNHA + BC Gaming + Province of BC. OSR includes rental, skills training, other income, royalties, and income from business enterprises. No schedule of remuneration included in the extracted text. Tangible capital assets net book value from Note 11: $52,443,113. COVID-19 noted as a subsequent event with unknown financial impact.
